content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 18: The argument ladder

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to creation and editing: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
STRUCTURING & ORGANISING | PUSH | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Build a stronger argument by separating claim, reason, evidence, objection, and reply.

THE SITUATION:
Choose something you need to persuade someone about. It can be small: a process change, a recommendation, a budget ask, a hiring decision.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Build a stronger argument by separating claim, reason, evidence, objection, and reply. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model to structure your argument as claim, reasons, evidence, likely objections, and replies.

2. Pick the weakest reason and ask the model to make the objection stronger.

3. Rewrite the argument so it no longer depends on the weak point.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- The argument has a clear claim rather than a cloud of related thoughts.
- At least one objection got stronger before your response got stronger.
- The final version would survive a sceptical reader.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to emails, updates, proposals, posts, reports, scripts, briefs, and all the places where a first draft normally arrives late wearing a suspicious hat.

COACHING NOTE:
Good AI use is not just acceleration. It is a way to expose weak thinking earlier, while it is still cheap to fix.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Write the first sentence as "I recommend..." to force commitment. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
review_notes: Generated as part of the 90-Day AI Habit paid founding track.
Continues from the free 14-day starter sequence without duplicating the foundation.
Organised by professional capability themes: creation, research, decisions, creativity, management, communication, productivity, analysis, workflow, judgement, and workplace adoption.
Draws from official prompt guidance and behavioural science principles without copying source programme content.
